Elizabeth R. Bain, Esq., of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9513), appearing for defendant / moving party Campbell Industries
Oren P. Noah, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Gary Hopster

RULING:
DEFENDANT CAMPBELL INDUSTRIES' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Campbell Industries' motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the substantial factor issue and off calendar as to strict liability, false representation and punitive damages. As to substantial factor, defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that plaintiff's exposure to asbestos-containing materials disturbed by defendant was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to an increased risk in developing his asbestos-related illness. Defendant's separate statement did not demonstrate that it propounded contention discovery seeking all facts, witnesses and documents regarding substantial factor causation or that plaintiff's responses thereto were factually devoid. Plaintiff dismissed his claims for strict liability and false representation and his request for punitive damages. Order signed in open court. RULING:
DEFENDANT FRASER'S BOILER SERVICE, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Fraser's Boiler Service, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to strict liability and punitive damages and off calendar as to false representation. Plaintiff's deposition testimony and declaration and Mr. Wilkerson's deposition testimony create a triable issue whether defendant's employees were in the vicinity of plaintiff, thereby precluding summary judgment in favor of defendant on "false identification," the sole issue for which defendant sought summary judgment. Defendant's objections to plaintiff's declaration and Mr. Wilkerson's deposition testimony are overruled. As to strict liability, defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant was a manufacturer, seller, supplier or distributor of products or otherwise placed products into the stream of commerce. As to punitive damages, defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant acted with malice, fraud or oppression. Plaintiff dismissed his claim for false representation. Defendant's objections to Mr. Hopster's deposition was overruled. Order signed in open court.
